title: Modelling of transport processes: Theory and simulations
author: Gupta, Pal, et al.

steps_raw.title: Analysis of TASEP with open boundary conditions
steps_raw.instruction: To scrutinize the system, we need to solve the Eqs.¬†(2)вАУ(4) that involves two-point correlators. Our motive is to obtain the explicit values for вМ©ѕДiвМ™вИАi which is intractable in the present form. Therefore, we utilize an approximation, known as mean-field theory (MFT), to break it into smaller correlators [29].

steps_raw.title: Analysis of TASEP with periodic conditions
steps_raw.instruction: We now discuss the effect of the periodic boundaries on the stationary properties of the system. Contrary to open systems, the number of particles N remains constant. Moreover, owing to the hardcore exclusion principle, N is bounded above by L. Since the dynamics in the bulk is similar to the open case, the master equation¬†given by Eq.¬†(3) remains unaltered. However, for the boundaries, the equations¬†are expressed as(38)dвМ©ѕД1вМ™dt=вМ©ѕДL(1вИТѕД1)вМ™вИТвМ©ѕД1(1вИТѕД2)вМ™,dвМ©ѕДLвМ™dt=вМ©ѕДLвИТ1(1вИТѕДL)вМ™вИТвМ©ѕДL(1вИТѕД1)вМ™.Here, we leave the discussion on transient solutions to the reader which can be contemplated analogously as done for open boundary conditions. The only change that arises in Eqs.¬†(14)вАУ(24) is the initial condition. Owing to the fact that the number of particles is conserved in case of periodic boundaries, the initial function g(x) must be chosen such that вИЂ01g(x)dx=N/L. For convenience, the value of NL is denoted by n.

steps_raw.title: Analysis of TASEP with periodic conditions
steps_raw.instruction: Using similar mean-field treatment as performed in the case of open boundaries, the master equations¬†for the boundaries (Eq.¬†(38)), in steady state reduces to(39)ѕБ(0)=ѕБ(1).Additionally, due to the number of particles being conserved,(40)вИЂ01ѕБdx=n,must hold.

steps_raw.title: Analysis of TASEP with periodic conditions
steps_raw.instruction: The particle density is controlled by the parameter n and can portray one of the three distinct phases: LD, HD, and MC depending on the number of particles. This dependence of the phases on n, where 0вЙ§nвЙ§1, can be represented in the form of a phase line (see Fig.¬†9). For n 1/2, the system is in the HD phase. If n=1/2, i.e., the system is exactly half-filled, the MC regime occurs.Fig. 9Phase line for the system with periodic boundaries representing the three phases. The particle density is regulated by the parameter n=N/L and is given by ѕБ=n. For the LD phase, ѕБ 1/2, and in MC phase, ѕБ is exactly equal to 1/2[37].Fig. 9

steps_raw.title: Analysis of TASEP with periodic conditions
steps_raw.instruction: Till now, we have analyzed the TASEP with both open and periodic boundaries through mean-field approach and obtained their steady state properties. To validate these theoretical outcomes, we first use Monte Carlo simulations with a random sequential update rule, followed by the parallel update.
